primary_change.factual_summary: Final pre-EOM-closure poll. All 6 covered dome-page hashes byte-identical. 9 commits in 11.94h, all auto-pipeline (7 status updates + 2 data pulls). 0 manual, 0 source-file SHA changes, 0 daily-AI-review regen this window. Manual-commit drought now 13.46d; 24 consecutive auto-only polls. Canary trap UNCHANGED (4 pre-existing .ots files, no prediction-isolation file, 0 canary-keyword hits in last 9 commits). All V51.1 parameters baseline-clean. PRED-105 predictions.html byte-identical for 22 consecutive polls (~10.36d).

kernel_of_truth.description: The substantive content of this change is the *absence* of dome-side activity at a moment when activity would be expected if the author were preparing for the imminent 8-PRED-EOM closure batch (closures at 2026-04-30T23:59Z, ~52 min after the poll). The poller correctly elevated this to MEDIUM substantive even though no bytes moved — silence is informative when contextualised against (a) the historical March-2026 closure-batch pattern (post-closure activity within 24-72h), and (b) the author-contact prior (DM contact 2026-04-26) which raised the baseline expectation that dome-side moves are seen and considered.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_matters: The next 24-72h decides which of two dome-author readings is correct: (a) deliberate batched-edit pre-staging that will surface as a post-closure rewrite; (b) sustained disengagement from active site curation. Both readings have plausibility; both have counter-evidence; neither can be falsified from this single quiet poll.

forensic_timeline.interpretation: Pure auto-pipeline cadence. No deliberate author touch on any branch since 2026-04-17. The 11.94h between polls captured a full normal day-cycle of automated commits with zero human signal.

strategic_assessment.classification: cadence-rollup quiet observation; pre-decision period for the post-EOM-closure adjudication question
strategic_assessment.threat_level: low (this poll); pivot diagnostic occurs at next 1-2 polls
strategic_assessment.author_contact_prior_application: NOT APPLICABLE to this change because there is no dome-side move to classify as responsive vs unrelated. The DM-contact prior elevates baseline only when dome-side surface activity exists; here the surface is byte-identical.
counter_arguments: Could the silence itself be interpreted as a deliberate response to our review (e.g. avoiding any post-closure goalpost moves while we are watching)? Counter: too thin to assert. The author has historically used auto-pipeline cadence as a default; one missed cycle is not evidence of strategic disengagement on review-related grounds.

Could the HOLD #9 streak (daily-AI-review not regenerating despite cron schedule) signal a deeper infrastructure issue rather than author choice? Counter: cron timing matches expected ~07:35Z firing; HOLD #10 is due ~07:35Z 2026-05-01 and will distinguish (a) cron working but registry stable -> HOLD continues, (b) cron broken -> ABSORB / no-fire, (c) cron fired but registry rewritten -> CRITICAL. We should not adjudicate the cron question from HOLD #9 alone.

Could there be a chance that author is actively responding to our content via channels we do not see (drafts, branches, private notes) that will land in a single batched commit later? Counter: GitHub API shows no non-main-branch activity, no draft commits, no force-push. The pre-staging hypothesis is supported only by historical pattern, not by visible artefacts.

recommended_actions_rationale: No analyst-actionable items. (a) Our review text is already correct on every load-bearing claim affected by this poll. (b) Adjudication of the silence question is premature — the next 24-72h will decide it, and the poller has explicit watch-list items for the post-closure poll, the HOLD #10 regen, and the canary triggers. (c) The prediction-tracking infrastructure is in place; the decider will re-assign PRED-090 / PRED-106 / PRED-105 for verdict updates per Mode 1b discipline once their windows are confirmed past-close on the next poll.

This run correctly cross-checked FUSE and acted on the fresher state.

External GitHub issue #1 has been canonically logged since 2026-04-28 (report-1-devilwench-friendly-meta.json). Initial assumption that it was unprocessed was wrong — the directory listing during my dispatcher trace did not show it because my clone was fresh and external-reports/ was empty in the clone (it is workspace-only / FUSE-only). Tombstoned my duplicate write on FUSE to avoid future confusion. Lesson: external-reports/ is FUSE-canonical, not git-canonical; check FUSE before writing.
